intro: Torilis stocksiana, also known as Stocks' hedge parsley or Torilis, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, menstrual cramps, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Torilis stocksiana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Torilis stocksiana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate menstrual pain, digestive issues, and skin irritations.
